preamble: The transformation of a voiceless aspirated velar stop [kʰ] into a voiceless aspirated retroflex affricate [ʈʂʰ]. This shift parallels the plain velar-to-retroflex shift in languages with robust aspiration contrasts.

examples.note: Historical development of retroflex aspirated affricates in certain phonological environments.

sources: Duanmu, S. (2007). The Phonology of Standard Chinese.
